reason_for_decision: Clear, technically believable, and especially strong at explaining field of view as a workflow transition problem rather than a camera spec debate.
strengths: Explains clearly why wide and narrow views serve different stages of the verification chain.
Connects cue error, scene ownership, stabilization, and reacquisition in a way that gives the topic real system-design depth.
Provides strong operator-facing value by framing FOV selection around trust, transition speed, and retained context.
summary_assessment: A strong EO verification article with good task framing, credible technical logic, and non-obvious insight into how field of view choices affect operator confidence.
